609	[Associator for $\otimes_X$ on invertible objects]
610	  \label{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}
611	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso}
612	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
613	        lem:flat_whisker_localizer}
614	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let
615	  \(M, N, P \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be \(\otimes\)-invertible
616	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), i.e.\ locally free of rank one.
617	  There is an isomorphism
618	  \[
619	    (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P
620	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
621	    M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)
622	  \]
623	  in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). This is an \emph{objectwise}
624	  existence-of-isomorphism statement for the three fixed invertible objects
625	  \(M, N, P\) --- not a natural associator on a category --- and that is
626	  exactly the datum consumed by the group law on \(\otimes\)-iso-classes
627	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}), where associativity is the
628	  proposition \(\mathrm{Nonempty}\bigl((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \cong
629	  M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\bigr)\).
630	\end{lemma}
631	
632	\begin{proof}
633	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
634	        lem:flat_whisker_localizer}
635	  The associator is built directly from the presheaf-of-modules monoidal data
636	  by absorbing the two nested sheafifications, with the only non-formal input
637	  being the flat-whiskering bridge \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer}.
638	
639	  \emph{Notation.} Write \(a = \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\) for the
640	  sheafification functor and \(M^{\flat}\) for the underlying presheaf of an
641	  object \(M\), so that the substrate of \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} is
642	  \(M \otimes_X N = a\bigl(M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}\bigr)\), with
643	  \(\otimes^{p}\) the presheaf-of-modules tensor product
644	  (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules}). Let
645	  \(\eta = \mathtt{toSheafify}\) be the sheafification unit, the canonical
646	  morphism \(A \to a(A)^{\flat}\); it lies in the sheafification localizer class
647	  \(J.W\), being locally injective and locally surjective
648	  (\(\mathtt{instIsLocallyInjectiveToSheafify}\)). Let \(\alpha\) be the
649	  associator of the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category,
650	  \[
651	    \alpha \;\colon\;
652	      (M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}) \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}
653	        \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
654	      M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} (N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}).
655	  \]
656	
657	  \emph{Flatness is free.} Since \(M, N, P\) are \(\otimes\)-invertible they are
658	  projective, hence flat (Mathlib
659	  \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible} \Rightarrow \mathtt{Projective} \Rightarrow
660	  \mathtt{Flat}\), via \(\mathtt{Module.Flat.of\_projective}\)). The flatness
661	  hypothesis of \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer} is therefore satisfied by each
662	  of \(M, N, P\) at no cost.
663	
664	  \emph{The nested form.} Unfolding the substrate on both sides exposes an inner
665	  sheafification:
666	  \[
667	    (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P
668	      \;=\; a\Bigl(\, a\bigl(M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}\bigr)^{\flat}
669	              \otimes^{p} P^{\flat} \,\Bigr),
670	    \qquad
671	    M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)
672	      \;=\; a\Bigl(\, M^{\flat} \otimes^{p}
673	              a\bigl(N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}\bigr)^{\flat} \,\Bigr).
674	  \]
675	  The required isomorphism is the three-step composite:
676	  \begin{enumerate}
677	    \item \emph{Absorb the inner sheafification on the left.} The unit
678	      \(\eta_{M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}}\) lies in \(J.W\); its
679	      right-whiskering by the flat object \(P\),
680	      \(\eta_{M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}} \triangleright P^{\flat}\),
681	      again lies in \(J.W\) by \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer} (\(P\) flat).
682	      Applying the sheafification functor \(a\) inverts it:
683	      \[
684	        a\Bigl(\, a\bigl(M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}\bigr)^{\flat}
685	              \otimes^{p} P^{\flat} \,\Bigr)
686	          \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
687	        a\bigl(\, (M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}) \otimes^{p} P^{\flat} \,\bigr).
688	      \]
689	    \item \emph{Transport the presheaf associator.} Apply \(a\) to \(\alpha\):
690	      \[
691	        a\bigl(\, (M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}) \otimes^{p} P^{\flat} \,\bigr)
692	          \;\xrightarrow[\ \sim\ ]{\;a.\mathtt{mapIso}\,\alpha\;}\;
693	        a\bigl(\, M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} (N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}) \,\bigr).
694	      \]
695	    \item \emph{Restore the inner sheafification on the right.} The unit
696	      \(\eta_{N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}}\) lies in \(J.W\); its
697	      left-whiskering by the flat object \(M\),
698	      \(M^{\flat} \triangleleft \eta_{N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}}\), again
699	      lies in \(J.W\) by \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer} (\(M\) flat). Applying
700	      \(a\) inverts it:
701	      \[
702	        a\bigl(\, M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} (N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}) \,\bigr)
703	          \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
704	        a\Bigl(\, M^{\flat} \otimes^{p}
705	              a\bigl(N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}\bigr)^{\flat} \,\Bigr)
706	          \;=\; M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P).
707	      \]
708	  \end{enumerate}
709	  Composing the three isomorphisms yields
710	  \((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \xrightarrow{\sim} M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\).
711	  Only steps 1 and 3 carry non-formal content, and that content is exactly the
712	  flat-whiskering bridge \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer}; step 2 is the image
713	  under \(a\) of the presheaf monoidal associator already supplied by Mathlib.
714	
715	  This is an objectwise isomorphism for the three fixed invertible objects, not
716	  a natural associator on a category: no pentagon identity is constructed or
717	  used. The group law on \(\otimes\)-iso-classes
718	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}) consumes only the existence of this
719	  isomorphism, \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\,\cdots \cong \cdots)\), so this is all that
720	  is required. The construction uses
721	  \emph{no} \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R)\),
722	  \emph{no} open-immersion restriction-compatibility isomorphism
723	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}), and \emph{no} per-object local
724	  trivialisation or gluing cocycle on an affine cover: the two absorption
725	  isomorphisms are obtained directly from flatness of the whiskering object via
726	  \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer}, without instantiating any bundled monoidal
727	  structure on the localizer class.
728	\end{proof}

1064	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR (Modules on Ringed Spaces,
1065	  ``Invertible modules''), Definition~01CX and Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-%
1066	  invertible}. The isomorphism classes of invertible \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
1067	  form an abelian group \(\Pic(X)\) under \(\otimes\), with unit
1068	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\) and inverse the dual; the tensor product of invertibles is
1069	  invertible and the dual is a tensor inverse.}
1070	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The isomorphism classes of \(\otimes\)-invertible
1071	  objects of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
1072	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}) form a commutative monoid under the
1073	  substrate tensor product \(\otimes_X\) in which every element is a unit ---
1074	  equivalently, an abelian group. Multiplication is

1077	  (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, which transports
1078	  \(M \cong M'\) to \(M \otimes_X N \cong M' \otimes_X N\) and symmetrically)
1079	  and the tensor product of two \(\otimes\)-invertibles is again

# Strategy

## Goal

Formalize Christian Merten's Jacobian challenge (`references/challenge.lean.ref`): the nine
protected declarations headed by `AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian` and
`Jacobian.nonempty_jacobianWitness` — an Albanese/Jacobian object uniform over the `k`-rational
pointing of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C/k` (`[Field k]` only; no
`C(k)≠∅`, no `CharZero`). `J := Pic⁰_{C/k}` is built unconditionally; only `isAlbaneseFor` is
quantified over the pointing. Spine: pointed vs. unpointed.

**Posture — option (c).** Forward the Route-A Picard substrate while Riemann–Roch stays frozen
by the USER ROUTE C PAUSE. The nine protected decls typecheck modulo named sorry-axioms (0
project axioms; none yet kernel-clean). RR-pause price: two RR-free obligations that are large
or unverified — the general Quot engine and Route-2 autoduality (see Open questions).

## Phases & estimations

| Phase | Status | Iters left | LOC (rem · /it) | Key Mathlib needs | Risks |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A.1.c.SubT — line-bundle ⊗-group law | active; gate cleared axiom-clean (211); associator scaffolded | ~2–5 | ~130–250 · ~50/it | sheafification-localization bridge `J.W f → IsIso(a.map f)`; `Units(Skeleton)` iso-class group | low: gate held; residual is plumbing, not structure |
| A.1.c — RelPic functor | held (placeholder bodies) | ~3–5 | ~50–150 · 0/it | A.1.c.SubT iso-class group; ét-sheafify on `Over S` | RE-ENGAGE: replace dishonest `PicSharp := const PUnit` + `functorial := 0` |
| A.2.c — Pic representability (scaffolding) | priority-2 | ~12–16 | ~600–800 · 0/it | A.1.c | `⟨sorry⟩` constructors discharged only by the engine below |
| A.2.c-engine — general Quot/Cartier (RR-free) | **HOLD pending USER RR decision** | ~30–60 | ~3400–5500 · 0/it | R^i f_* (i≥1), Relative Proj, CM-regularity, Grassmannian, flattening | largest build; 2–4× cheaper RR route exists behind the pause |
| A.3.0/ii/vii — tangent + Pic⁰ AV-structure | gated A.2.c | ~26–45 | ~1100–2100 · 0/it | scheme tangent space; Hilbert poly | absent in Mathlib; likely under-counted |
| A.4 — Albanese UP (Route 2) | gated A.2.c; autoduality RR-freeness UNVERIFIED | ~12–20 | ~600–1000 · 0/it | `rmk:Alb` + autoduality `J^∨≅J` + Galois descent | if autoduality needs RR, collides with paused Route C |
| genusZero + witness body | gated A.3 | ~5–7 | ~250–450 · 0/it | tangent-iso + connectedness | hidden A.2.c transit |

**Total Route A**: ~90–170 iters / ~4000–7000 LOC (RR-free engine path).

## Routes

`J := Pic⁰_{C/k}` (Kleiman §4–5, Nitsure §5, Milne III §6). Bottom-up (USER): ungated roots
first, no gated target before its roots, no A.3+ before A.2.c. Every directive cites
Kleiman/Nitsure/Milne/Mumford/Hartshorne/Stacks. **Critical path (RR-free):** A.1.c.SubT →
A.1.c → A.2.c.

**A.1.c.SubT — group law via ⊗-invertibility.** Pic = `Units` of the monoid of ⊗-iso-classes of
invertible `Scheme.Modules` (mirrors `CommRing.Pic`; predicate `∃ N, M⊗N≅𝒪`, Stacks 01CR).
Iso-class axioms are propositions, so only the four existence-of-iso lemmas are needed — no
coherent monoidal category, no `MonoidalClosed`. Unitors/braiding via `sheafification.mapIso`
(done); associator via flat-exactness whiskerLeft on the localizer `J.W g→J.W(F◁g)` (done,
axiom-clean), whose remaining input is the bridge "sheafification inverts a `J.W` morphism".
Local-trivialization rejected (the absent restriction wall).

**A.2.c — representability + Quot fork (held).** Six Prop-valued typeclasses with `⟨sorry⟩`
constructors scaffold representability (~600–800 LOC); Route A proceeds under them. Discharge
fork: RR-free general Quot/Hilbert engine (Nitsure §5 + Kleiman §4, ~3400–5500 LOC, all
Mathlib-absent; deepest root `R^i f_*`, i≥1) vs cheap curve route (Kleiman §5, `Sym^n`/Abel–
Jacobi, ~600–1000 LOC, needs paused RR). Engine HELD behind A.1.c.SubT→A.1.c.

**Albanese UP — Route 2.** UP from `Pic` representability via Kleiman `rmk:Alb` (RR-free) on the
dual `J^∨`, landed on `J` by autoduality `J^∨≅J` (Milne 6.6 / EGK 2.1) + Galois descent `k̄→k`
(Milne 6.4). Supersedes the Route-1 codim cone, retained reversibly with the deletion gate
closed until autoduality RR-freeness is re-verified (theta divisor rests on RR — top risk).

**Route C — Riemann–Roch — PAUSED (USER).** Imported with inline sorries (option c). Needed at
the three Goal nodes; would unlock the cheap curve route. Pause cost: the ~3400+ LOC engine and
the autoduality risk exist solely to avoid RR.

**Genus-0 arm.** (a) Route-A Pic⁰-via-AV-wrap (transits A.2.c); (b) direct `J := Spec k` via
Mumford rigidity — substrate partial, PAUSED (USER).

## Open strategic questions

- **A.2.c — pay for RR or build the RR-free substitute (USER decision, priced):** keep pause ⇒
  ~3400–5500 LOC Quot engine + autoduality risk; lift ⇒ ~600–1000 LOC `Sym^n`/Abel–Jacobi slice.
- **Autoduality `J^∨≅J` RR-freeness** (EGK 2.1 / Poincaré bundle): second-verify before any
  Route-2 investment — classically RR-dependent; if it transits RR the RR-free posture fails.
- **`k̄→k` Galois descent** at the no-`C(k)` heart: verify per-pointing `isAlbaneseFor` composes
  with descent + autoduality before treating it as minor.
- **`R^i f_*` (i≥1) treatment** (gates the engine): Mathlib PR vs project Čech build (~800–1200)
  vs named typed-sorry pin. Decide when the engine de-gates.

## Mathlib gaps & new material

**Gaps to fill (Route A).**
- A.1.c.SubT: sheafification-localization bridge `J.W f → IsIso(a.map f)`; `Units(Skeleton)`-
  shaped CommMonoid/group on ⊗-iso-classes (~130–250).
- A.1.c: ét-sheafify on `Over S`; `OnProduct` re-point to the `IsInvertible` subtype.
- A.2.c-engine (HELD): `R^i f_*` (i≥1), Relative Proj, geometric Hilbert poly, CM-regularity,
  semi-continuity, flattening, Grassmannian, Quot representability, relative Cartier (~3400–5500).
- A.3 / A.4: scheme tangent space, Hilbert poly, Pic⁰ AV-structure; `rmk:Alb` UP, autoduality,
  Galois descent.

**New project material.** AbelianVarietyRigidity, RigidityLemma, Genus0BaseObjects/*, RiemannRoch/*
(paused), Picard/{RelativeSpec, LineBundlePullback, RelPicFunctor, FGAPicRepresentability,
IdentityComponent, Pic0AbelianVariety, QuotScheme, FlatteningStratification, TensorObjSubstrate},
Albanese/AlbaneseUP. Route-1 cone retained reversibly behind the closed deletion gate.
